action: Investigate cycle 31 ship-integrity breach: commit 55d1dc5 message claims c38 script changes (inbox-audit.sh NEW, inbox-reconcile.sh NEW, inject-task.sh --injected-by/--force/--help, inject-task-test.sh +55 LoC = ~155 LoC across 4 files) but actual git show --stat HEAD shows ONLY 11 .evolve/inbox/*.json additions, zero script changes. The c38 script edits sit uncommitted in project tree as dirty M files for scripts/utility/inject-task.sh and scripts/tests/inject-task-test.sh — orphaned. Audit-report claimed PASS based on worktree content; ship.sh captured a different tree-SHA. state.json:lastCycleNumber did not advance past 30. Investigate: (a) did Builder write to project tree instead of worktree, (b) was the worktree out-of-sync at audit→ship boundary, (c) did ship.sh's tree capture miss untracked-yet-staged files, (d) was audit-bound SHA verification bypassed. Deliverables: docs/incidents/cycle-31-c38-orphan.md with full forensics from .evolve/runs/cycle-31/ (first attempt, started 02:56:08Z), ship.sh hardening so audit-bound SHA must equal committed tree-SHA, and recovery plan for the orphaned dirty files (re-ship via --class manual or full cycle re-execution).
operator_note: Operator-flagged. CRITICAL: Triage's idempotency Step 0a (c37) may incorrectly route this to skip_shipped[] if it only matches commit subjects against keywords like 'c38' or 'inbox-audit.sh'. Before claiming skip_shipped, verify the commit ACTUALLY contains the promised file changes via git show --stat. If only state-mutation paths (.evolve/inbox/*.json) were committed but build-promise paths (scripts/utility/inbox-audit.sh, scripts/utility/inbox-reconcile.sh) are absent, the prior cycle did NOT actually ship the work. This task BLOCKS v9.4.0 release-pipeline.sh — preflight.sh will fail on dirty tree, and the v9.4.0 changelog would falsely advertise c38 features that don't exist in HEAD.
